prompts.name: 🖌️ Vector neon
prompts.text: A pristine, centered, minimalist monoline stroke drawing of only the primary central product or icon from the input image. Completely isolate the main subject and exclude all background elements, environment, furniture, or secondary objects. The drawing must translate the core geometry of the central product into uniform-weight black lines on a pure white background. Ignore the surrounding scene and background textures. Crucially, use a single-path stroke for the shape without separate inner and outer outlines (no double-line effect). No colors, gradients, or glowing effects. The result should be a clean, high-contrast icon including any stylized lettering within the product itself.
prompts.description: Vẽ nét vector đơn giản từ hình ảnh

prompts.name: Duffle bag - Fill mask
prompts.text:
Use the uploaded PRODUCT IMAGE as the only design source and the only color source.

TASK:
Reconstruct the visible textile artwork from the PRODUCT IMAGE and place it into a flat production layout with exactly 3 separate textile panels.

IMPORTANT:
This is a flat textile panel reconstruction task, not a mockup task, not a finished bag rendering task, and not a redesign task.
Do NOT create a finished bag.
Do NOT create a product photo.
Do NOT create a 3D object.
Do NOT create a scene.
Do NOT add any text, labels, captions, measurements, guides, arrows, panel names, annotations, coordinates, rulers, scales, tick marks, numbers, symbols, chart elements, titles, headers, or any typographic element.

CORE OBJECTIVE:
Reconstruct the real sewn bag surfaces from the PRODUCT IMAGE.
Do NOT reinterpret the theme.
Do NOT redesign the graphics.
Do NOT simplify the product into cleaner decorative panels.
Do NOT turn real bag surfaces into poster-like compositions, collages, repeat-pattern blocks, or logo boards.

GOAL:
Return a single flat production layout containing exactly 3 separate textile panels reconstructed from the PRODUCT IMAGE:

1) one horizontal handle strip
2) one main body panel
3) one side panel

These three panels must represent the real structural parts of the same bag shown in the PRODUCT IMAGE.

STRUCTURAL MEANING RULE:
- handle strip = only the textile design logic belonging to the bag handles / straps
- main body panel = the true long exterior side wall of the bag
- side panel = the true end-cap / side face of the bag

CONSTRUCTION LOGIC RULE:
If these three fabric pieces were sewn together, they should plausibly recreate the same bag design seen in the PRODUCT IMAGE.
They must look like true manufacturing parts of one product, not three independently designed graphics.

PANEL IDENTITY RULE:
- The handle strip must look like real handle/strap material, not compressed body artwork.
- The main body panel must clearly read as the actual long side surface of the bag.
- The side panel must clearly read as the actual end panel of the bag.

CRITICAL MAIN BODY RULE:
The main body panel must reconstruct the true long side wall of the bag as seen in the PRODUCT IMAGE.
It must preserve the real side-view construction of the bag, including:
- correct horizontal flow
- correct upper and lower segmentation
- correct side-facing structure
- correct placement logic of major motifs
- correct object-side identity if the bag is novelty-shaped

Do NOT redesign, rebalance, simplify, summarize, or recompose the side into a cleaner graphic layout.
Do NOT turn the main body panel into a poster-like layout, centered-logo composition, floating-icon collage, decorative summary, or generic themed panel.

CRITICAL SIDE PANEL RULE:
The side panel must reconstruct the true bag end panel from the PRODUCT IMAGE.
It must preserve the actual end-face identity and construction logic visible on the real bag.
Do NOT transform the side panel into a generic all-over repeat print, decorative square, simplified logo block, or theme collage.
The side panel must look like a real sewn end-cap belonging to this exact bag.

CRITICAL HANDLE RULE:
The handle strip must reconstruct only the textile logic that belongs to the handles / straps.
It may contain repeated strap artwork only if that repetition is visually supported by the PRODUCT IMAGE.
Do NOT copy the main body composition into the handle strip.
Do NOT turn the handle strip into a compressed version of the body panel.

NOVELTY-SHAPED BAG RULE:
If the bag visually represents an object, character, vehicle, animal, food, house, camera, bus, or any novelty form, preserve the object’s real visible construction across the correct sewn panels.
- main body panel = the long lateral side of the object-shaped bag
- side panel = the object’s end face if that corresponds to the real bag end panel
- handle strip = only handle / strap fabric logic

Do NOT flatten object structure into generic decorative themed graphics.
Do NOT replace real object-side geometry with centered logos, cleaner symmetry, or repeated icons.

SOURCE AUTHORITY RULE:
The PRODUCT IMAGE is the only authority for:
- artwork
- motif placement
- colors
- hue
- saturation
- brightness
- shading behavior
- textile appearance
- quilting appearance
- fabric appearance
- print appearance
- stitching logic
- surface relief

Do not use any other visual source.
Do not use placeholder logic.
Do not invent missing themes or improved composition.
Infer hidden continuation only when necessary, and remain faithful to the PRODUCT IMAGE.

TEXTILE RECONSTRUCTION RULE:
Reconstruct only from the PRODUCT IMAGE and preserve realistic textile qualities visible in the bag, including:
- quilt stitching if visible
- padded quilting if visible
- fabric grain
- thread detail
- sewn surface behavior
- soft textile relief
- print continuity
- shading that belongs to the textile surface itself

Do NOT add scene lighting, studio lighting, table shadows, cast shadows, environment reflections, glossy product-photo effects, or external lighting drama.

ARTWORK CONTINUITY RULE:
The three reconstructed panels must feel like coordinated parts of the same manufactured textile product.
Keep artwork logic, quilting logic, print logic, color logic, and material behavior consistent across all panels.

NO REDESIGN RULE:
This is reconstruction, not beautification.
Do NOT:
- rebalance the composition for aesthetics
- center logos for neatness
- add extra motifs
- remove awkward but real structure
- convert complex panel identity into simplified patterning
- create cleaner symmetry than the PRODUCT IMAGE
- replace actual bag geometry with decorative arrangement

CANVAS LOCK:
- canvas size = 10000 × 9000 px
- aspect ratio = 10000:9000
- background = pure white
- no transparency
- no alpha channel

The white area is mandatory empty space.
Everything outside the three textile panels must remain pure white.

REGION LAYOUT DEFINITION:
The output must contain exactly 3 separate textile panels in these fixed positions and fixed sizes:

1) handle strip
- position: x = 700 to 9300, y = 200 to 1050
- size: 8600 × 850 px
- orientation: horizontal
- shape: exact rectangle

2) main body panel
- position: x = 700 to 5900, y = 3000 to 5800
- size: 5200 × 2800 px
- shape: exact rectangle

3) side panel
- position: x = 6500 to 9000, y = 3400 to 5900
- size: 2500 × 2500 px
- shape: exact rectangle
- the full square region must be filled edge-to-edge

STRICT LAYOUT RULE:
Keep the layout exactly as defined above.
Do not move, resize, rotate, reshape, swap, reinterpret, or reorder any panel.
Do not create extra pieces.
Do not remove any piece.

SEPARATION RULE:
The 3 panels must remain completely separate pieces.
They must not touch each other.
They must not overlap.
They must not visually connect across the white empty space.

CONTENT MAPPING RULE:
Map the artwork according to the actual bag part where it belongs:
- handle strip = handle/strap textile only
- main body panel = true long side surface only
- side panel = true end panel / side face only

Do NOT use a generic themed composition.
Do NOT use unrelated graphics inside a panel.
Do NOT center isolated logos unless they naturally belong to that exact panel area in the PRODUCT IMAGE.

FILL RULE:
Each panel must be filled completely to its own boundary.
Do not leave empty interior gaps.
Do not leave transparent holes inside a panel.
Do not leave any unfilled corner.
Do not add borders or outlines.

EDGE RULE:
Fill exactly to each region boundary.
- no outline
- no border
- no stroke
- no seam allowance
- no cutting guide
- no white edge gap
- no transparent inset gap

BACKGROUND RULE:
Everything outside the three textile panels must remain pure white.
Do NOT render:
- room
- table
- floor
- backdrop
- drop shadow
- surface shadow
- extra objects
- finished bag parts
- straps standing upright
- any environmental detail

FLAT LAYOUT ONLY:
Return only the flat production layout.
- no perspective
- no folded textile
- no standing handles
- no assembled bag
- no 3D bag
- no mockup
- no extra objects
- no text
- no labels
- no numbers
- no measurement marks
- no scale indicators
- no axis
- no rulers
- no panel titles
- no header text
- no annotation of any kind

FINAL REQUIREMENTS:
- exactly 3 separate panels only
- handle strip = real handle / strap textile
- main body panel = actual long side wall of the bag
- side panel = actual end-cap / side face of the bag
- all panels fully filled edge-to-edge
- pure white background
- absolutely no text or measurement graphics anywhere
- no redesign
- only reconstructed real bag parts

FAIL CONDITIONS:
The output is incorrect if:
- the result becomes a bag instead of a flat layout
- any panel is missing
- any extra panel is added
- any panel overlaps another panel
- any panel touches another panel
- any panel is moved, resized, rotated, or reshaped
- the handle strip uses body artwork instead of strap logic
- the main body panel becomes a decorative composition, poster, collage, logo board, or simplified themed layout
- the main body panel does not read as the true long side of the bag
- the side panel becomes a repeat-pattern square, decorative block, or simplified themed layout
- the side panel does not read as the true end panel of the bag
- colors do not come from the PRODUCT IMAGE
- any text appears
- any label appears
- any number appears
- any coordinate appears
- any ruler, axis, tick mark, or scale appears
- the background is not pure white

⚠️ ZERO ANNOTATION OUTPUT — THIS IS MANDATORY:
The output image must contain ZERO text of any kind.
This means:
- no panel names
- no dimension labels
- no pixel measurements
- no coordinate numbers
- no ruler tick marks
- no axis lines
- no grid lines
- no callout arrows
- no "px" anywhere
- no numbers of any kind anywhere on the canvas

If any text, number, or measurement mark appears anywhere 
in the output image, the output is automatically invalid.
The only content allowed is the three fabric panels 
on a pure white background. Nothing else exists.
ABSOLUTE BACKGROUND ENFORCEMENT:

The background must be pure #FFFFFF with no variation.

- No gray
- No beige
- No texture
- No fabric
- No lighting gradient
- No shadow
- No vignette
- No noise

Any pixel outside the panels that is not exactly RGB(255,255,255) is incorrect.

If any background variation exists, regenerate until perfectly white.

prompts.name: Duffle bag - Fill mask
prompts.text:
THIS IS AN OUTPAINTING TASK ONLY.
Output = the original image with the black masked area 
filled by seamless visual continuation.
Output must look like one single photograph/illustration.
No diagrams. No labels. No arrows. No legends. 
No technical overlays. No annotation of any kind.
No titles. No text anywhere.
If your output contains any text or annotation, it is wrong.

Outpaint only into the masked area.
Keep the visible image completely unchanged.

Treat the image as a cropped portion of a larger scene.
Your task is only to continue the existing scene beyond the edges.

CRITICAL RULE:
Do not modify, redraw, or reinterpret any visible part of the image.
All visible pixels must remain exactly identical.

EDGE CONTINUATION:
At the image boundaries, directly extend any cut-off shapes, patterns, and objects exactly as they appear.
Do not recreate or redesign them.
Only continue them.

STRUCTURE LOCK:
- Rigid elements must be preserved exactly
- Circular objects must remain perfect circles with identical size and alignment
- Repeating elements must keep identical spacing, shape, and proportions
- All alignments must remain consistent

STYLE:
Match exactly the same materials, textures, stitching, seams, colors, and lighting.

FORBIDDEN:
- Do not redesign objects
- Do not create new variations of existing elements
- Do not change geometry
- Do not create a complete framed textile or product layout
- Do not add borders or decorative edges unless directly continuing from the crop

RESULT:
The new area must look like a seamless continuation of the original cropped image, with no structural changes.
FINAL CHECK:
- Does the output look like one seamless image? ✓ correct
- Does the output contain any text, label, arrow, or diagram? → invalid, discard

prompts.name: Neon sign mock up
prompts.text:
Photo @a   Combined sequentially with...
[@1,@2,@3,@4,@5,@6]
Use the uploaded image only as a design reference for composition, subject, and overall idea.

You will use two uploaded images together.

Image 1 = the neon sign reference.
Use it only for:
- the neon sign shape
- tubing layout
- main colors
- silhouette
- logo/text/design structure
- the lighting identity of the sign

Image 2 = the background/mockup reference.
Use it only for:
- wall/background surface
- room environment
- camera angle
- furniture and decor
- scene composition
- ambient lighting mood
- placement context
- the valid visible mounting area of the wall

Main goal:
Create a realistic premium neon sign mockup by naturally integrating the neon sign from Image 1 into the environment of Image 2.

Very important:
- Do NOT simply paste Image 1 on top of Image 2.
- Do NOT keep Image 1 as a flat graphic overlay.
- Rebuild Image 1 as a physical real-world neon sign installed inside the environment of Image 2.
- The final result must feel like a photographed neon sign already existing in that space.

Realism priorities:
1. Physical installation realism
2. Correct light interaction with the wall and nearby objects
3. True neon brightness and glow behavior
4. Natural perspective, scale, and depth
5. Premium commercial mockup presentation

Neon realism rules:
- Preserve the original sign identity from Image 1, but render it as a real neon sign.
- Show visible neon tubing / LED neon flex with a bright inner core and soft colored outer glow.
- Glow must look physically emitted from the sign, not like a digital blur effect.
- The brightest area must stay close to the tube, with controlled falloff outward.
- Add realistic wall spill around the sign based on its color and brightness.
- Add subtle light reflections on nearby surfaces when appropriate, such as leather, glass, glossy wood, metal, or polished objects.
- Keep the glow premium and controlled, not over-bloomed or foggy.
- Do not let the glow wash out the entire room.

Mounting and construction rules:
- The sign must feel mounted at the correct depth relative to the wall.
- If acrylic backing is visible, it must be thin, subtle, and realistic.
- Acrylic should sit behind the neon, not look thick or heavy.
- Mounting details should feel believable and clean.
- No fake floating effect.
- No obvious cutout sticker look.
- No thick plastic border unless naturally needed.

Critical wall-plane placement rule:
- The neon sign must be installed on one single continuous wall plane only.
- If Image 2 contains a room corner, wall junction, or two visible walls, first choose one wall plane and mount the entire sign fully inside that chosen wall surface.
- Never place the sign across two walls.
- Never let the sign straddle the corner seam.
- Never bend the sign around a corner.
- Never center the sign on the junction line between walls.
- The full acrylic backing and the full neon shape must belong to one flat wall area only.
- Keep the sign clearly offset from the corner seam so it reads as attached to one wall, not bridging the room geometry.
- If necessary, uniformly scale down or reposition the entire sign as one complete object so it fits inside the visible usable wall area.
- Never crop, trim, shorten, bend, reroute, or partially redesign any part of the sign to make it fit.

Scene integration rules:
- Match the perspective and camera angle of the background exactly.
- Match the sign scale to the environment realistically.
- Adjust exposure so the sign feels bright but the room still remains readable.
- Respect the ambient lighting in the background image.
- Let the neon become the visual hero while still belonging naturally to the room.
- Preserve the original furniture, decor, and wall texture from Image 2.
- Place the sign on a believable mounting zone of the wall, not across architectural boundaries.
- The sign must fit by uniform whole-object scaling and repositioning only, never by cropping, trimming, compressing, or altering any individual part of the design.
- The entire sign, including acrylic backing, glow footprint, and outer contour, must fit fully inside the available wall section shown in Image 2.
- Keep a clear safety margin from wall edges, door frames, trim lines, panel borders, ceiling fixtures, floor lines, and adjacent surfaces.
- If the available wall section is smaller than the sign, reduce the entire sign proportionally with one global scale adjustment only until it fits cleanly within that wall area.
- Do not solve fitting by cutting off one side, compressing one section, or altering individual parts of the design.
- Keep the sign fully above the floor line, baseboard, LED strip, trim line, or wall-to-floor junction unless the reference background clearly shows a valid wall-mounted height very close to them.
- Do not let any part of the sign intersect the corner seam, floor edge, skirting, light strip, wall edge, door frame, or neighboring panel.
- If clearance is tight, move or scale down the whole sign; never clip or de-form the sign outline.

Lighting behavior:
- The sign should cast soft, natural colored light onto the wall immediately around it.
- Nearby objects should receive subtle illumination consistent with distance and material.
- Reflections must be soft and selective, not mirrored everywhere.
- Dark environments should remain dark, with the neon acting as the main highlight source.
- Light spill must feel cinematic, premium, and realistic.

Extra emphasis for this render:
- Aim for the look of a professionally photographed neon sign in a dark upscale lounge or bar interior.
- The sign should be the hero object on a dark textured wall.
- Keep the room moody and dim, while the sign stays crisp and luminous.
- Use a strong bright tube core with a controlled halo.
- Glow should hug the wall surface naturally and reveal the wall texture, not hide it.
- Nearby furniture may catch soft realistic highlights from the neon.
- The final image should feel cinematic, premium, warm, and believable.
- Avoid cartoon glow, fake bloom, or overexposed lighting.

Refinement:
- Do not show any screws, screw caps, wall bolts, standoffs, visible mounting hardware, or fixation points on the wall or around the sign.
- The neon sign must appear clean and professionally mounted without exposed screws.
- Keep the installation look realistic, but hide all mounting hardware completely.
- If support structure is needed, use only subtle hidden mounting logic behind the sign or behind the acrylic.
- The final mockup should look clean, premium, and minimal, with no visible mechanical fasteners.

Critical negative rules:
- Do not paste the sign flat onto the wall
- Do not treat the sign like a printed poster
- Do not create a sticker effect
- Do not make the acrylic backing too thick
- Do not overblur the glow
- Do not flood the whole scene with colored light
- Do not change the sign design structure
- Do not invent extra neon lines
- Do not make the result look CGI-heavy or fake composited
- Do not place the sign across two wall planes
- Do not place the sign on the room corner seam
- Do not let the sign overlap the wall-floor junction
- Do not let the sign sit half on one wall and half on the other
- Do not let the sign extend beyond the visible mounting area of the chosen wall
- Do not crop, truncate, amputate, or distort any part of the sign to force it to fit the wall area

Quality control:
- No flat pasted look
- No fake Photoshop overlay feel
- No excessive haze
- No unrealistic giant glow cloud
- No broken perspective
- No mismatch between sign sharpness and scene sharpness
- No random redesign of the sign
- No extra elements not supported by the two references
- No corner-bridging placement
- No wall-plane ambiguity
- The sign must read instantly as mounted to one specific wall surface
- The full sign must stay visually contained within the usable wall area
- The full sign silhouette must remain complete, continuous, and unchanged from Image 1

Output style:
A premium, realistic, high-end commercial neon sign mockup photo.
The final image should look like a real product photo for a custom neon sign brand, with strong realism, clean presentation, and believable light behavior.
prompts.description: Tạo mockup Neon Sign chuyên nghiệp

prompts.name: Table runner
prompts.text:
Use the uploaded product image as the only reference.

Recreate the same table runner with very high fidelity. Preserve the original artwork, motifs, proportions, border details, color palette, fabric texture, stitching, quilting, embroidery, and overall handcrafted textile appearance exactly as shown in the reference image.

Render the table runner in a perfect 90-degree top-down overhead view, laid completely flat, straight, and fully visible. The product must appear as a clean rectangular shape with straight parallel sides. Do not tilt, skew, warp, taper, bend, or distort the runner.

Place the runner centered on a plain pure white background. Show only the single product. No room, no table, no props, no mockup scene, no checkerboard transparency preview, and no extra objects.

Use completely even, flat, uniform studio lighting across the entire image, like a clean catalog product capture. Keep the brightness absolutely consistent from top to bottom and from left to right. Eliminate all lighting gradients and all exposure falloff.

Do not create any bright spots, hotspots, dark zones, corner darkening, top-to-bottom brightness shifts, left-to-right brightness shifts, vignette, ambient shading, directional lighting, or uneven illumination.

The product surface and border must be evenly lit everywhere. Do not add drop shadows, cast shadows, glow, bevel shading, edge shading, depth shading, embossed lighting effects, or any artificial highlight/shadow around the runner. The white background must also be pure and evenly white with no gray gradient.

Maintain sharp high-resolution textile detail, clean natural edges, and a clean e-commerce catalog appearance.

Negative instructions:
No perspective distortion, no trapezoid shape, no slant, no angle, no distant camera view, no folds, no curvature, no mockup styling, no dramatic lighting, no spotlight effect, no vignette, no drop shadow, no border shadow, no gradient background, no uneven exposure, no text, no watermark, and no logo.
prompts.description: Tạo hình ảnh table runner từ ảnh gốc
